reviews.review_description: If 15-year-old me knew that someday he'd be able to play a Square, tactics game in virtual reality, he'd be completely floored. That's exactly how I feel when I see the game grid in the middle of my living room. Absolutely amazing and I hope there is more of this to come from Square and others in the future.

Such a great game! I have to admit the controls do feel a little off compared to other titles but you soon get used to them. Also the early game is slow (for a good reason though) but once it gets going it’s so engaging.

developer_response.body: Thank you for taking the time to write this review! We're thrilled to hear that you enjoyed the game and hope you continue to have fun playing it!

reviews.review_description: Triangle Strategy is an incredibly rich VR experience perfect for those wanting to mix grounded, high-tension narrative with addicting strategic gameplay. The type of experience that really shines in VR/MR!

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing your positive experience with Triangle Strategy! We hope you have fun exploring the game further!

reviews.review_title: No cross save but really cool in VR
reviews.review_description: Argh, this is a tough one for me. The game is actually a really cool experience in VR. But there’s no cross-save with other platforms, and I just don’t think I can handle 80 hours in VR for this. My suggestion would be that Square allow cross save with other platforms (whether Steam or even just Quest PC) so that you can occasionally throw on the headset for a treat or big battle, but be able to play the rest of the game - especially the lengthy cut scenes which are 2D in VR anyway - elsewhere. It would get me to double dip, but I think as it is, I’ll have to refund.

developer_response.body: We appreciate you taking the time to share your thoughts with us. Your feedback is invaluable in helping us improve and grow.

reviews.review_title: A greate game. I hope they make more!
reviews.review_description: A game with a deep story and just the right level of difficulty. It's meant to be played for several months, following the entire narrative and the development of the characters you choose. After finishing it, it's an RPG that leaves you longing for more. I hope they make more games in this style. Technically, I would like to suggest to the developers the possibility of tilting the battlefield view, so that you have an overhead view without having to tilt your head so much.

developer_response.body: Thank you for taking the time to write this review! We're thrilled to hear that you enjoyed the game and hope you continue to have fun playing it!

reviews.review_description:
I've been playing Square RPGs since I was a kid. While this title has the expected Square story depth, it lacks the exploration that Square RPGs are known for. You can't wander a large world and explore, finding hidden items, side quests, secret bosses, etc. And grinding gets boring since the only way to grind for levels and materials is to re-fight battles you've already fought. 

Visually it is good. The usage of 3d controls is innovative. The story is engaging. As far as Quest games go, it's great. But measured against the lineage of Square RPGs, it's middle of the pack.

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing this feedback with us. We hear you and we will keep this in mind going forward.

reviews.review_description:
I don't normally go for sedentary games in VR, but a 30% off sale offered this cheaper than other platforms, so I gave it a try. And...yeah. It works. The 3D battle and exploration maps that you can rotate and raise/lower are quite fun. The combat system is engaging. Just be warned that the story takes a *significant* amount of time to get going. So long that you likely won't see more than the intro battle in your first session before having to recharge. But after the first few chapters, the pacing gets much better. I only wish Square Enix had found better voice actors. It's not like they don't have the money for it...

Updated to five stars from four. What I said above is true, but the game improves *substantially* later on. And then you can skip (or at least speed up) the into in subsequent playthroughs if you want. The voice acting also either gets better or ends up growing on you. Can't quite tell, ha.

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing your positive experience with Triangle Strategy! We hope you have fun exploring the game further!

reviews.review_title: Need more games like this in VR!
reviews.review_description: Awesome game and the mixed reality is excellent. It feels like you’re playing a board game on your table and the cutscenes look as if they’re projecting on a big screen theater. Brilliant game and awesome VR conversion. Would love to see Octopath Traveler Series games in VR.

developer_response.body: Thank you for taking the time to write this review! We're thrilled to hear that you enjoyed the game and hope you continue to have fun playing it!

reviews.review_description: Great game!

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing your positive experience with Triangle Strategy! We hope you have fun exploring the game further!

reviews.review_description: I applaud Square Enix for porting anything to VR. I hope that they consider porting even more games in the future because they make good games, and VR adds needed novelty to their catalog. My issue is that they have no consideration for left-handed gamers. This is an accessibility option that most VR developers worked out through the early days of VR. It's one thing to use a mouse or gamepad with the right hand, but asking lefties to point with VR controls is a different story. It's doable but far from ideal.

developer_response.body: We appreciate you taking the time to share your thoughts with us. Your feedback is invaluable in helping us improve and grow.

reviews.review_description:
Thank you for the advise on tilting function!
Other than this, I hated nothing about the game.
Rather loved it!
Thank you!

------------------------------------
Very well-made game, but adapted to VR by someone who doesn't know VR much.

There is no TILT function. My neck is about to break.

It really really need 'tilt' function.
Looking down with VR on your head is causing super pain on your neck.

For safety reason, please PLEASE update 'tilting' function. I really want to play, but neck is killing me..

developer_response.body:
Thank you very much for your valuable feedback.

We apologize for any misunderstanding, but please note that this product is not designed to require users to keep their heads tilted downward continuously.

First, please review the boundary settings on your Meta OS.
If these settings are incorrect, the game board may appear below your body, which can lead to neck strain.

If the issue persists, try resetting your viewpoint by pressing and holding the OS button.
This should resolve the problem.

Additionally, we'd like to introduce a new feature of Meta Quest designed to reduce neck strain and enhance comfort.
With this feature, you can enjoy games even while lying down.
How to enable this feature in Meta Horizon OS:

1.Open Settings
2.Select Advanced Settings from the side panel
3.Enable Use apps while lying down
Once enabled, the device will switch to horizontal display mode.
At that point, look upward and press and hold the OS button to recalibrate the display.
※ To use this feature, Quest 2/Pro must be updated to v63, and Quest 3/3S to v64.

Steps to launch TRIANGLE STRATEGY:
・After launching the game, look upward and press and hold the OS button
・Perform the same action when waking the device from sleep

We hope this feature helps make your gaming experience more comfortable and enjoyable.

reviews.review_title: wow what a performance art and show
reviews.review_description: let's see so if i was gonna see i wanted a game that reminded me of zelda a play performance and final fantasy all originals mind you this would be the game. far beyond this of course is the sheer satisfying performances. look i'm not saying everything's perfect like the map could use a bit more substance or thickness to it but honestly just about everything else, is really good game play, even has a sense of urgency and puzzling to how to dual it out.  a game that is spot on fantastic from which you can tell there was a lot of love and excitement to put this together and really the effort is throughout the whole experience. it's that platformy kind of face down but looks fantastic close up or from a far. there's that word again because let's face it it's a five star

developer_response.body: Thank you for taking the time to write this review! We're thrilled to hear that you enjoyed the game and hope you continue to have fun playing it!

reviews.review_description:
Walking the balance between choose-your-own-adventure novel, and table-top-rpg, Triangle Strategy rewards introspection and discipline. The story is an engaging and digestible middle/dark ages romp with magic, history, and valor, and suprising heart. The CriWare team that put together impressive jrps in Octopath 1 and 2 continue their solid work here, bringing bigger, slower, fewer, and more emotionally hefty tactical battles that encourage players to master the rules before applying creativity. 

The hook in the game is in the plethora of morally grey and implication-laden choices the player must make along the way. Ranging from the undignified (do you tell the boy in the villiage town surrounded by war that he can't play far from the house because of the  unpredicable conflicts of 'great men'? or just tell him that he must listen to his parents? or that you'll have him to you manse at wars' end?) to the most perilous for the realm. Each choice deserves careful thought, and although i'm not sure how it will end, I know the game is scoring and sculpting my "convictions" along the way. The fact that they aren't telling me how I'm scoring up, either in the game's three themes of morality, utility, and liberty, but also in whatever other metric (there do seem to be some 'hey, are you smart enough to see through this or not' questions) they have makes me endlessly curious to see how my actions with affect Novaria.

I love it. I hope you try it if it seems interesting to you

developer_response.body: Thank you for taking the time to write this review! We're thrilled to hear that you enjoyed the game and hope you continue to have fun playing it!

reviews.review_description: I think that if you’re not big on graphics, the core gameplay and story are definitely there. I have been a major fan of square Enix since 1997 and I feel like all the elements that I’m looking for in the game are already there. The only real problem is the graphics for me. It ended up being an eyesore and I was starting to enjoy the story And the battle mechanics are pretty much on point. The only thing is it’s hard to look at. ￼

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing this feedback with us. We hear you and we will keep this in mind going forward.

reviews.review_title: Square Enix Scored big with this one!
reviews.review_description:
This is a great VR adaptation of a turn-based RPG. It leans very heavy into the story, which I like, and the mechanics and look of the game are superb.

I hope this leads to Square Enix making more dives into VR. I would absolutely love a Kingdom Hearts VR game.

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing your positive experience with Triangle Strategy! We hope you have fun exploring the game further!

reviews.review_description: Wonderful strategy role playing game, I really hope to see more like it and more jrpgs in general ported the vr.

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing your positive experience with Triangle Strategy! We hope you have fun exploring the game further!

reviews.review_description: The game barely works. Takes an hour just loading the game, idk how the game is but ive owned it since launch and havnt been able to play its Sept 2025 cant make a game right?

developer_response.body:
We have identified that the game may reinitialize cached data upon startup following a firmware update. As a result, the expected pop-up screen may not appear correctly when launching the game.

We have confirmed that the game will still launch successfully after a short wait. Please try waiting approximately three minutes after starting the game to see if this resolves the issue.

For more details, please refer to the following post:
https://www.meta.com/experiences/app/developer-post/926159392986952/

If the problem persists, we kindly ask that you contact our support center via the following link:
https://support.na.square-enix.com/main.php?la=1&id=19192

We apologize again for the inconvenience and hope this helps you continue enjoying Triangle Strategy.

reviews.review_description:
I love these games since the days of old snes ff3, secret of mana, and what not.
I really want to adore it, the graphics are really nice for an oldtimer like me, the sound is great. The dialogue really makes this a story to fall in love with.
And then there is the controls....dont get me wrong everything works all the standard VR controls are there, manipulating the game vr wise works great...just playing the game takes loads of patience and to be fair they made it far too complex for a vr console machine to actually know what you are doing. 
Ya the learning curve is steep, so steep i need to relearn the game every time i start it up and turn it off out of frustration and restart it after a few weeks.
So at the moment the story is dead to me for the above reason. And again i turned it off. I hope i missed a skirmish mode in the menu so i can practice the weirdest thing.

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing this feedback with us. We hear you and we will keep this in mind going forward.

reviews.review_description:
I was a huge fan of this game when it released on the Nintendo Switch, and seeing it ported to the Quest was one of the best surprises I've seen on the platform.

Triangle Strategy makes excellent use of VR for perspective, and the motion controls transform the game into point-and-click affair which works beautifully.

The game offers an absolutely massive campaign with multiple endings and characters to unlock, allowing for plenty of replay value.

This is easily one of my favorite strategy games of all time (I actually prefer this to Final Fantasy Tactics) and I hope that Square decides to release more RPGs on the quest.

SquareEnix, please bring Dragon Quest, Octopath Traveler and more classics over next!

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing your positive experience with Triangle Strategy! We hope you have fun exploring the game further!

reviews.review_description: I love RPG games especially from Sqaure Enix. This is the first time I've played a game from this series and I'm loving the nostalgic vibe. A traditional game on the Quest is a breath of fresh air. I don't always feel like jumping around and being in first person. I do look forward to similar experiences like maybe a Final Fantasy title. Anyway I'm more than happy with this game and recommend for any old school RPG fan.

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing your positive experience with Triangle Strategy! We hope you have fun exploring the game further!

reviews.review_title: Bad VR Implementation of a Great Game.
reviews.review_description:
So sad that I have to rate this game so low. I am a huge proponent of VR and JRPGs, so the prospect of a venerable AAAcompany such as SquareEnix releasing a JRPG in VR was a godsend to hear (I purchased immediately in support, and in hopes for more JRPGs to get ports to VR). 
Unfortunately, this game fails horribly in key areas of VR implementation, that it can only be interpreted as a rushed/cashgrab port.  While the original game at the core of this port is excellent (story, music, gameplay, etc), the VR portions suffer major technical and presentation issues that make the game unenjoyable (...and that is very hard for me to write as a VR & JRPG fan).
TECHNICAL:
- ASW/Reprojection is horrible (Quest 3). The lag and ghosting of any motion (ex. controllers, game objects, etc.) is so bad in VR mode, it leaves behind a visible double image that completely muddies text and images. This is very nausea inducing for novice VR users. Oddly, in MR mode, the ghosting/lag is gone (I can only guess that ASW is not enabled in MR mode?). There's absolutely no reason VR mode needs ASW for this simple gfx game.
-Resolution is too low (Quest 3). I understand trying to maintain performance across Quest2 & 3, but the low resolution utilized causes shimmering and missing pixels that render text illegible (on top of the ASW ghosting). Again, there's no reason a Quest 3 cant handle a bump to native resolution for this simple gfx game.
PRESENTATION:
- 2D cut scenes. The biggest letdown for this game was the 2D window for the cutscenes.  The whole point of VR is the feeling of being present in the game environment. A 2D video of a 2D game in a 2D window is a triple slap in the face for VR users....and borderline false advertising as none of the game trailers show the usage of a 2D cutscene window.  The cutscenes should have been rendered in the same way as the actual gameplay (3D field with 2D sprites). 
BOTTOM LINE:
The game needs better VR platform optimization and VR implementation.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback. We are sorry to hear that the experience did not meet your expectations. We will be sure to share your feedback with the development team.

reviews.review_description: Buy, Buy ,buy ,buy ,buy a great purchase 👍🏿

developer_response.body: Thank you for taking the time to write this review! We're thrilled to hear that you enjoyed the game and hope you continue to have fun playing it!

reviews.review_title: Love genre. Very underwhelming VR Port
reviews.review_description:
HUGE fan of Final Fantasy Tactics. Upon seeing Triangle Strategy releasing to VR, I was so keen to get started, expecting an immersive turn based/levelling game, with the expected common traits of a game similar to FFT. Unfortunately, the game in the first 2 hours is 98% cutscenes (in 2D, by the way) with unconvincing voice overs. I was not immersed in the story and spent a long time skipping dialogue just to get to the 'play' part of the game. The buttons also do not work all of the time consistently on Quest 2, particularly when trying to perform actions before movement. The game seems to strongly encourage movement before action.
If this game had smooth consistent controls, that worked the same every time. Quick pace turn based, and more battle, less cutscenes (or actual VR cutscenes) , this could be game of the year. Unfortunately it feels like a quick port, with very little embedded VR features. It has the ability to be so great, but there's no reason for this title to be in VR because the port did not have enough put into it, there's counter intuitive immersion. Why not just play on a Nintendo portable, or steam deck instead? Very underwhelmed, and not eligible for refund because I spent too long in 2D cutscenes! Hope this improves.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback. We are sorry to hear that the experience did not meet your expectations. We will be sure to share your feedback with the development team.

reviews.review_description: I’ve played everything you can imagine. This is by far my favorite game, and on VR is incredible.

developer_response.body: Thank you for taking the time to write this review! We're thrilled to hear that you enjoyed the game and hope you continue to have fun playing it!

reviews.review_description: Good and fun game,just like having Final Fantasy in VR. VR needs more games like this

developer_response.body: Thank you for taking the time to write this review! We're thrilled to hear that you enjoyed the game and hope you continue to have fun playing it!

reviews.review_description: I don’t understand the few negative reviews here. I think those people never played this type of game. The controls are not hard got used to them rather quick in my first battle. The introductory is not overly long and you are in a battle before long. This is a great tactical game and if you love these type of games and love vr then why wouldn’t you get this? The price was great also. Only thing I question is the default battle dificiulty being set to very easy. Changed it to normal as I like a challenge. Just no you may want to change that early on. Keep em coming like this please.

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing your positive experience with Triangle Strategy! We hope you have fun exploring the game further!

reviews.review_description: Updated Review: Game was previously unplayable due to patch issues. Was able to get it resolved finally after 2 months of in-game startup errors. You MUST keep headset on for the 5-10 minutes while it is stuck on the black screen loading loop. If you take off the headset it pauses & the broken loading screen will not resolve. No pop-up opened for me either notifying about the fix, so would recommend devs update their troubleshooting instructions.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback on this issue. Could you please register with our support center to investigate this issue? A support representative will get back to you. https://support.na.square-enix.com/contact.php?id=19192&la=1 We appreciate your cooperation.

reviews.review_description:
The battles look great; even have a lot of dialogue in them. The cutscenes are such a cop-out. 
Still pretty good though

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing this feedback with us. We hear you and we will keep this in mind going forward.

reviews.review_description:
I've been thoroughly engaged with Triangle Strategy VR, and the transition to VR significantly enhances the experience compared to the Switch version. The immersive environment and enhanced visuals truly bring the game to life. However, the experience is marred by significant issues with the Meta 3 controllers.

Firstly, the controllers frequently lose tracking, causing the laser pointer to disappear, which disrupts gameplay. Additionally, the maps often load inside the headset, resulting in a black screen until I manually adjust them by reaching up and pulling them down into view. This is an unnecessary hassle that breaks immersion.

In-game navigation is also problematic. Even the slightest movement can cause your character to unintentionally turn in the wrong direction, which is frustrating during critical gameplay moments. Furthermore, the game unpredictably switches between pointer and non-pointer modes, adding to the confusion.

While the VR adaptation has potential, these control issues are too significant to overlook and have led me to lower my overall score for the game. The developers need to address these problems to fully realize the game's potential in VR.

developer_response.body: We appreciate you taking the time to share your thoughts with us. Your feedback is invaluable in helping us improve and grow.

reviews.review_description: Before you can experience this in VR, expect to spend 30-40 minutes in menus and watching a series of story videos that are being shown on a virtual flat screen, with no VR benefits. Most story is told in pre rendered scenes this was. And for the first several hours, you spend more time watching these than doing anything in VR. VR stuff is vlfun though, and at least the story is good.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback. We are sorry to hear that the experience did not meet your expectations. We will be sure to share your feedback with the development team.

reviews.review_description:
I like it a lot, it's very cool .. nostalgic 

Only thing I don't like is the lack of the ability to use the left controller. 

Other than that it's very worthwhile

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing this feedback with us. We hear you and we will keep this in mind going forward.

reviews.review_title: Exactly what I'd hoped it would be like!
reviews.review_description:
This is *exactly* what you'd hope for out of a Strategy RPG game done up in VR. It looks amazing, and plays great. The visuals (especially on a Quest 3 with "Quest Games Optimizer" running to notch up the graphics settings) are wonderful, and sitting through cutscenes with plenty of dialog is a comfortable/pleasant experience, not something you feel like you've got to "endure" to get back to the gameplay.

The 'pass-thru' mode is the coolest freaking thing ever. If you're into SRPGs, and you've got a VR headset, you pretty much owe it to yourself to give this a spin, IMO.

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing your positive experience with Triangle Strategy! We hope you have fun exploring the game further!

reviews.review_description: Was having the loading issue. After v74 update and reinstalling , I got the pop up for the extra content to download finally and was able to play!

developer_response.body: We're grateful for your feedback and appreciate the time you took to report this issue. Your input is invaluable in helping us improve the gaming experience for everyone.

reviews.review_description:
This game has gotten me hooked on VR turn-based RPGs. What they have done using the VR form factor—as well as mixed reality—is amazing.

Triangle Strategy does a great job with world building and has a huge cast of unique characters. The battles give you so much freedom in approaching fights, and remind me of great old school games like Shining Force. The levelling up and upgrade system also allows you to customize a lot. 

I hope they keep releasing VR RPGs, I will buy every one of them. Including re-releasing any older games.Thank you Square Enix!

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing your positive experience with Triangle Strategy! We hope you have fun exploring the game further!

reviews.review_description:
The default resolution is way too low on the quest 3 making text hard to read and everything else really hard to watch and enjoy. Please bump up the resolution on quest 3! No reason for it to be this low!
Also, why do the cutscenes have to be on a flat 2D screen? Wouldn’t it be possible to make it a stereoscopic 3D screen?? And why can’t I bring that 2D screen closer so I would be less distracted by the horrible text aliasing. This feels like a really lazy port and this games deserves more.

(P.s: if you’re going to get it then you can increase the resolution for free using Sidequest)

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback. We are sorry to hear that the experience did not meet your expectations. We will be sure to share your feedback with the development team.

reviews.review_description: Absolutely awesome, beautiful graphics and satisfying fights. Interacting with cats makes them meow. I strongly recommend using QGO to improve resolution though, since there is a lot of text and by default it looks a bit jaggy

developer_response.body: Thank you for taking the time to write this review! We're thrilled to hear that you enjoyed the game and hope you continue to have fun playing it!

reviews.review_title: Great game but VR port can be better
reviews.review_description: I spent 80+ hours playing this game in VR/MR. It's definitely a great journey for me. The game is well made and triggered me a lot of thoughts by making tough decisions. While there are different opinions on Reddits, I found the protagonist Serenoa's English voice acting is super touchy. I like the texture of his voice, so as other English voice acting. However, this just means that it's originally a very good game. The VR port, however, can be much better. There is a lot of aliasing on the graphics, for not only UI but also environment. I know the game is pixel art style but it can be done better to make the characters more dimensional than just a sprite? When the narrator speaks, the scroll displaying the animation feels super cheap and the fidelity can be much better than that. I haven't played this game on 2D but from the playthrough, I feel the graphics blend much better on 2D screen than in VR. A lot of button use on the VR controllers which feels very console like rather than designed for VR, but it brought something new to me to get used to, so not that bad. I really hope this VR port could have done better, so more people can enjoy the game.

developer_response.body: We appreciate you taking the time to share your thoughts with us. Your feedback is invaluable in helping us improve and grow.

reviews.review_description: Games fabulous as a concept. That being said the controls are far too confusing even with the tutorial explanations it's just too little too much at once. However I'm getting a refund simply because of that alone. The control complexity of the game. It's too complicated for something supposed to be so simple. 3* best you get. As I loved the concept I was seriously looking forward to this then the whole control layout during especially combat way too complicated for this.

developer_response.body: We appreciate you taking the time to share your thoughts with us. Your feedback is invaluable in helping us improve and grow.

reviews.review_description: Can't wait to have more games like this on quest

developer_response.body: Thank you for taking the time to write this review! We're thrilled to hear that you enjoyed the game and hope you continue to have fun playing it!

reviews.review_description: Full disclosure - I loved FF Tactics when it came out, but this game surpasses that game easily. The story, navigation, interface, and graphics are amazing. I love the strategy table in AR. I have a 100 or so games on quest, and this is by far my favorite title. Well worth the money.

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing your positive experience with Triangle Strategy! We hope you have fun exploring the game further!

reviews.review_description:
Square we are begging you to give us a final fantasy in vr!! Or at least more RPGs:)

This game is so good!! Get it if you haven’t!!

It utilizes MR as well and is a work of art. You won’t be dissapointed if you’re a fan of tactics games.

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing your positive experience with Triangle Strategy! We hope you have fun exploring the game further!

reviews.review_title: Takes way too long with initial load
reviews.review_description: I’ve sat looking at the loading screen for what feels like forever and I’ve never even seen the title screen. I don’t know if this is a glitch they are patching or what. Right now, not playable. I have reset my system, I have deleted and re-downloaded the game, and I made sure I have the latest software update. Still nothing. Would change review if that changes. ￼

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback. When you first start the app, a pop-up will appear telling you that you need to wait 3 minutes while the resource data is loaded. We have also confirmed that the app starts up without any problems after a 3 minutes wait after updating the OS to v74. Please wait 3 minutes after starting up the app and check if it starts up. (You can also take off the device and wait during this time.)

reviews.review_description: Good port, works well in vr. When you enter a battle it works really well and looks good. Would love to see Square Enix port more games over.  Decent price for what you're getting 👌

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing your positive experience with Triangle Strategy! We hope you have fun exploring the game further!

reviews.review_description: The game has amazing potential, but it has one FATAL flaw: all of the cutscenes and dialogue are in 2D!! Why make a VR adaptation if I'm going to spend half of my time watching a flat screen??

developer_response.body: We appreciate you taking the time to share your thoughts with us. Your feedback is invaluable in helping us improve and grow.

reviews.review_title: Some things shouldn't be in VR
reviews.review_description: Would love this game on PC or console, but the controls are so clunky and easy to make mistakes. Super frustrating when tactics are key and you end up pointing your guy in the wrong direction almost half of the time because the headset is clipping with some terrain and the angle isn't right. If you could tilt the play space towards you for more of a top down it would be less prone to errors. The game is still ok. Although the graphics quality on the characters is something I feel like you'd see in the old virtual boy. They're basically 2-D images superimposed on the battlefield. Story is solid, it's just lacking in playability and graphics.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback. We are sorry to hear that the experience did not meet your expectations. We will be sure to share your feedback with the development team.

reviews.review_title: I wanted this to be good. Its not.
reviews.review_description: Cant read the map text. Can't tilt world for comfort. Other reviews say it's 2 hour till I start fighting, draining the 2 hour window for refund. So I'm gonna request refund right now.

developer_response.body: We apologize for the inconvenience. You can move the world map board forward, so move it in front of the player and check the text up close.

reviews.review_description: So boring... the dialogue is awful and it never ends. I'm just clicking through every word hoping to get to something else. The game tried introducing a voting system, what??? Even the battles are drawn out and kind of lame. I keep playing it like it's about to get better but it hasn't yet. Side quests show up and I turn them all down because they're pointless. I've never been so disappointed in a Quest game before.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback. We are sorry to hear that the experience did not meet your expectations. We will be sure to share your feedback with the development team.

reviews.review_description: So I've never played this game and I believe that this is a console only game based on the return policy that quest has. With the first two hours there is only one opportunity to actually "play" the game. If I was forced to choose to return the game on that, I would have, but that doesn't give the game justice on that short time. Advise only playing this on console unless you already know about this game. And I would not even give a star rating jus to give the game fair opportunity, but as a quest game I'd say it's fair for new players that don't know the game.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback. We are sorry to hear that the experience did not meet your expectations. We will be sure to share your feedback with the development team.

reviews.review_title: It’s games like this that make VR great
reviews.review_description:
Im not even a full hour into the game. Just experiencing the battle mechanics, roaming mode, camping mode, etc. the voiceovers and the detailed drawings of the characters bring it all to life. 

The mixed reality makes it so surreal. Experiencing this game as a part of this world. It’s as if I’m in my own Isekai. 

I hope you guys continue to develop more games like this!

I would love to see Octopath Traveller in the future Square Enix, please do it.

reviews.review_title: High quality strategy RPG for VR.
reviews.review_description: This is an awesome strategy RPG that is similar to Final Fantasy Tactics or Vandal Hearts. First of all, this puts you in the world in a way that we could only imagine when we were kids. I can grab the map zoom. It really close to my face, rotate the map for a better view. This is the best strategy RPG on VR right now; Yes, it's better than Demeo for single player.  My Quest 3 is having a really awesome problem of too many good games.

reviews.review_description: The graphics are really cool, not always dark and brooding like Demeo. The best part is that it’s single player so I don’t have to worry about questing with a bunch of annoying real people. Really cool MR also with the game world right in the middle of my room and very easy to move it around without ever having to get up. I also like how the cutscenes open in a giant scroll on my wall. Awesome 50+ hour game.

reviews.review_description: Great game but mine just now loads forever without starting the game. 200 GB free on the headset. Tried restarting the headset and uninstalling/reinstalling. It is still suck at the loading screen and unplayable.

developer_response.body: Have you updated the firmware to v71? When you update the OS, the cache data will be re-downloaded, so please wait for about 3 minutes on the title screen, just as you did when you first started the game. After that, you will be able to visit the world of Norzeria again. Good luck on your journey!

reviews.review_description: This game won't work on quest3 or 3s. The devs need to look into this. Avoid buying this game if you have a quest 3 or 3s.

developer_response.body:
We have confirmed that Quest 3 and 3S are compatible with the latest firmware. We have also confirmed that the game is still running correctly.
After updating to v72, the initial resource download will occur again, but we have confirmed that the game will start up without any problems. Please wait for about 3 minutes, as you would when starting the game for the first time, and then check that the game has started up.

reviews.review_description: I reinstall 3 times game stuck at loading.

developer_response.body:
We have found that the game might reload the cache data when it starts up after the firmware update. The pop-up screen is not displayed as it should be when the game starts. We apologize for the inconvenience caused. 
We have confirmed that the game will still start up without any problems after waiting for a while. Please try waiting for around three minutes after starting the game to see if this resolves the problem. We apologise for any inconvenience and hope this helps you to continue to enjoy Triangle Strategy!

reviews.review_description: It's very story driven. If all you want is to get to the next battle this is not the game for you. You'll fight in the beginning then it'll be awhile till your next fight

developer_response.body: We appreciate you taking the time to share your thoughts with us. Your feedback is invaluable in helping us improve and grow.

reviews.review_title: Great game, could be totally awesome
reviews.review_description:
Tldr : I wish this game had hand tracking.
If you play on quest3 use Quest game optimizer it will make the game so so so much more smooth and readable, and I doubt the dev will ever update it for Q3

I've been longing for something like this, I'm really happy we're getting a VR port of a pixel tactical rpg!

Game itself it's pretty nice, great game system, great story amazing japanese voice acting, there is very little I dislike about it. So let's move on to the VR bits

The passthrough option is a very nice addition, and control need some time to get used to but overall it's very playable.
I find the overall rendering of the text a bit dirty, it has aliasing and visual is not that crisp, probably due to oculus limitation but for a game that need you to read this much it could be disturbing on long session.
I think this is the kind of game that desperately need to make use of hand tracking, not sure if we will ever get this but one can only dreams

developer_response.body: We appreciate you taking the time to share your thoughts with us. Your feedback is invaluable in helping us improve and grow.

reviews.review_description:
UPDATE: Changing this to a 4 star, as once you bump up the resolution with QGO then my first issue is gone (this game can easily do 200% resolution without issues, they need to add a Quest 3 patch!). The only issue now is the 2D flat cutscenes, if they updated those to have depth then it would a 5 star. The only reason it's enough of an issue to dock a star is because it's actually a significant portion of the game, it's not just a cutscene here or there. Overall though, amazing game and would love to see more like it!!

Original review: 

For now giving this 3 stars, but hopefully 5 in the future with updates.

The 2 big issues are:

- Low resolution/shimmering on Quest 3: This could definitely run at a much higher resolution, the text hurts my eyes currently 
- Cutscenes are 2D and not VR. This is a wild choice, it's fine to have them be in a window or whatever but have at least a 3d effect on the cutscenes themselves.

Additionally I do find the controls a hit awkward but I think I just need to get used to it. I'm mainly here to support Square and hope they do more VR releases in the future. Other than the VR specific issues the game seems amazing.

reviews.review_title: A classic strategy jrpg in diorama form
reviews.review_description: It's the version of a "Pixel Remaster HD2D" I've dreamed about but in 3D. A living strategy jrpg in diorama form. An excellent port of an amazing game.

Story is very well done and has multiple ways to experience it. This is a very story driven game, but if you’re just here for the action, you have the option to skip the scenes.   

The top down strategy works in VR. 

Overall I think this is the best tactics game in vr right now.
Great version of this game, it has mixed reality mode, buy to support the game, please Square bring more games to Quest

reviews.review_description:
I’m 33 hrs in so far in Chapter 9, Part II. It’s a brilliant 9.5/10 game that I will break down as follows:

Art – 10/10
Graphics – 9/10 (Using QGO on Quest 3)
Music – 10/10
Sound (and Voice Acting) – 9/10
Gameplay - 9/10
Story – 10/10
 
Because I've enjoyed this game so much, here are my notes for the devs, in order of importance. Hopefully they will read and consider for a sequel.

*Needs a tilting function. This game should allow tilting the 3D maps up to prevent having to constantly look down. (Like Demeo’s tilting function). This should be easy to patch in. I can see why they would not offer a zoom function given that we have 2D bitmaps mixed in, but there is no excuse for not having a tilt function.
 
*3D maps are reused too much. I feel like in a game like this we should revisit a map only once or twice in the entire game. Ideally never. It makes the world seem much smaller. Oddly I never saw any reviewer complain about this. The dream is to have 3D maps be randomly generated from art assets based on locations and a set of rules that varies with the battle scenario. (Play Spelunky for random level generation perfection).
 
*All the dialogue choices are too vague in terms of what the expected outcome can be. I like my choices to be more clear cut. I’d like to be able to choose between being selfish, compassionate, or reasonable. Most of the time you are given 3 reasonable choices, so it’s not clear what you can expect from a particular choice.
 
*A lot of time is spent in cutscenes and in 2024 we are used to seeing our avatars show their current equipment when rendered with in-engine cutscenes. The problem is the cutscenes are pre-rendered. But even in the 3D exploration, which IS done in the 3D engine, the characters don’t show their upgrades.

*Character portraits should be visible for the character speaking always. I should not have to press a button to see a portrait.
 
These nitpicks aside this game is brilliant. Great job team!!!

developer_response.body: We appreciate you taking the time to share your thoughts with us. Your feedback is invaluable in helping us improve and grow.

reviews.review_description: I was expecting much more from a square enix game, but Instead it feels like a game made with a RPG maker by a group of teens, bad voice acting, hard to tell the difference between characters, no epic music scores that you would expect from square enix,

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback. We are sorry to hear that the experience did not meet your expectations. We will be sure to share your feedback with the development team.

reviews.review_description:
If you're a fan of JRPG tactical turn based strategy games such as Final Fantasy Tactics or Fire Emblem, do yourself a favour and buy this game. This is the full length tactical RPG game that's worth its price tag. The developers did a good job porting the visuals to VR - it's a gorgeous game - though I wish they made the game interactions more tactile a la Demeo. Note that the game supports MR so you can bring the 3D battle into our living rooms. My only gripe is that the MR setting is not saved so I have to manually enable it every time I launch the game.

If you'd like to learn more about the game before buying it, go check out the game reviews for the Nintendo Switch version.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your feedback. We have shared your feedback with the development team and confirmed that it does not depend on the pass through setting in MetaOS, and that if the game's internal setting is ON, it should be ON the next time you start the game. Your input is very valuable in helping us improve the gaming experience for everyone. If the issue is not resolved, please contact customer support.
https://support.jp.square-enix.com/main.php?la=0&id=18892

reviews.review_description:
Tactical RPGs are few and far between, so it is very refreshing to have a game that took lessons from past greats (FFT, Fire Emblem, Shining Force) and made something this enjoyable.

Ported into VR, the game gains a few charming VR immersion moments, like when a rainstorm breaks out in a cutscene, the rain itself is a VR effect, instead of happening in the cutscene viewer.

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing your positive experience with Triangle Strategy! We hope you have fun exploring the game further!

reviews.review_description: I was pleasantly surprised that this game had parts in it where you can explore towns. Yeah sure the story parts are in “2D” but I don’t see how that’s a big deal. There is no other game quite like this. And I hope it’s a sign of things to come. Would love to see other square games ported. Maybe one of the older final fantasy games. Sure seems possible now. It’s for sure worth the money it’s a full fledged game.  Ive played a few hours so far and it’s fantastic. If you like strategy games then for sure recommend. Sometimes it’s nice to sit down while playing vr.

reviews.review_title: Interesting combo of new and old
reviews.review_description: I have only finished the first few chapters, but so far enjoy it very much.  Very deliberately paced and lots of reading, very different from any other Quest native app I have tried.  I play in pass through mode (about as new a style of game as there is) and it is very much old school pixel graphics (could have looked the same last millennium).  The cut scenes, of which there are many, play out on a scroll that unrolls in front of you.  The tactical/movement sections are on "boards" floating in front of you.  It's actually the first game I ever prepurchased as I would love to see more top publishers port turn based games (looking at you Larian :)) to the Quest, and I haven't regretted buying it for a minute.  Definitely give it a go if you like old school turn based RPGs and want a break from first person action.

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing your positive experience with Triangle Strategy! We hope you have fun exploring the game further!

reviews.review_description:
I love VR, but I have a tough time committing to games. The problem is that I don’t get a lot of time to play, so when I do, I quickly lose attention when I have so many games at the push of a button. Often, I’ll end up watching a movie instead.

Although I enjoy working out, I am also a lazy gamer. I’d rather sit and play than jump or move around. So I was excited about this game because it seemed like I didn’t have to do too much.

I’ve only played about four hours of this so far, but it’s just about the perfect game for me. The graphics are stunning; I’ve always been partial to pixel art, but pixel art in 3D is next level. So far, there’s a lot of exposition, but that’s ok… I feel like I’m watching an episode of Game of Thrones, and as someone who enjoys watching movies and tv in my headset, I’m here for it.

The story is growing on me, and I look forward to playing this more than any other game right now, including Batman. That’s not a knock on Batman, but rather a testament to what they have built here: a game that’s great in VR, but especially great for lazy gamers like myself. If you’re someone that fits this description, then I highly recommend! 11/10!!

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing your positive experience with Triangle Strategy! We hope you have fun exploring the game further!

reviews.review_description:
Meta, please reach out to Namco... I would die for Tales of Phantasia in this format 🥹 PLEASE 🙏🙏🙏

Hopefully Square Enix doesn't stop here either... that FF4 game they did in the 3DS would be wowzers in the stereoscopic 3D of the Quest. 😎

Other than that, I wish resolution was a wee bit higher... and couldn't you bundle all those logos in the startup screen together? Time-to-fun, peepz, and important thing to keep in mind ☝️

Other than that, love love love this, thank you Square/Meta! ❤️❤️❤️
